PLAYBOOK · 6th-Gen Quote Sheet
Start on Plus · Sell from the top down 1 / 21

🔥 Cook County Cooks · C³ Sales Playbook 2026

The Plus-First
Playbook

The simple, proven way to use the 6th-Gen Quote Sheet. Start every customer on Xfinity Mobile Plus, show the savings side by side, and keep room to close. This is the play, step by step.

Press → or Space to begin · F for fullscreen
THE BIG IDEA

Always sell from the top down.

We start every customer on Xfinity Mobile Plus, our best plan. Starting at the top gives the customer the most value, and it gives you room to make a deal if they hesitate.

⬆️

Start at the top

We lead with Plus because it has the best benefits. Plus also unlocks the most promotions, like device deals and trade-ins.

🎯

Keep your room

Starting high means you can still move if price is a worry. If you start low, you have nothing left to give.

🤝

Close and keep more

Because you always have a next offer, you win more sales. You also keep more customers happy for the long run.

We never start low. We start on Plus and protect our room to close. 🔥
THE PLAY · START TO CLOSE

Six moves, the same way every time.

Run this same play on every customer. The sheet walks you through it in order, step by step.

STEP 1

Find core savings

Show what they save on internet and TV by bundling with mobile.

STEP 2

The mobile pivot

Ask four simple questions to learn their current mobile plan.

STEP 3

Build the intake

Type in exactly what they have: carrier, lines, bill, watches, tablets.

STEP 4

Start on Plus

Quote our best plan first, every time.

STEP 5

Add devices

Add phones, trade-ins, watches, and tablets.

STEP 6

Build & recap

Show the side-by-side T-chart, then stop and ask.

THE LADDER

Close from the top

Offer Plus, then overcome the real objection, then the Select one-year offer.

FALLBACK

Save them anyway

If they pass on mobile, still save them on core with a watch, tablet, or home-phone line.

1 Find Core Savings First

Start with the money they already save.

Intake · Core Savings
1

Current Monthly Bill

Ask what they pay now for internet, TV, and the rest. Type it in.

2

Core Bill After Rebundle

This is what that same package costs once they add Xfinity Mobile. The sheet shows the Total Core Savings for you.

3

The Repackage panel

Pick their current internet and TV, then pick their new one. When the new one is better, the sheet adds a green UPGRADE badge. Now they get more for less.

Pro tip · Leading with savings earns the right to talk mobile.
2 The Mobile Pivot

Earn the talk, then ask four simple questions.

After they feel the core savings, switch to mobile. Don't pitch yet — get curious. Their answers fill in the sheet for you.

"There's another discount you qualify for. It's our mobile discount. Who do you have your cell phones with?"
"How many lines do you have? What does your cell phone bill cost you each month? Why do you pay so much?"

Why these four?

Each question fills a box on the sheet — carrier, number of lines, and monthly bill. The last question, "Why do you pay so much?", gets the customer to admit their bill is too high before you show a single number.

Carrier Current CarrierLines Number of LinesBill Current Monthly Bill
3 Build the Intake — Apples to Apples

Mirror their account. Exactly.

Quote what they have today — nothing more, nothing fancy. If they have 5 lines, 2 watches, and 3 tablets, you quote 5 lines, 2 watches, and 3 tablets.

Intake · Current Carrier
1

Carrier, Lines & Bill

These are the three required fields. They are the heart of the comparison.

2

Watches & Tablets

Add the ones on their current plan so the comparison reads line for line.

3

Membership Tier

Set it early (Silver to Diamond). The tier unlocks promotions later. For example, Diamond opens the $300-off smartwatch offer.

Don't upsell here · Just rebuild their world accurately.
4 Always Start On Plus

Quote Plus first — every single time.

Plus is our best plan. It gives the customer the most benefits and unlocks the most promotions. Starting here is the heart of top-down selling.

Intake · Xfinity Plan
🛡️

Protection included (XMC)

XMC means Xfinity Mobile Protection. On Plus it's built in, so a cracked screen won't mean a surprise bill.

⚡

Better, faster data

Plus gives premium unlimited data, so it stays fast even when the network is busy.

📶

Hotspot + 4K streaming

Customers can share their data and stream in the sharpest quality.

🎁

The big promotions live here

Device deals and trade-ins only work on Plus. Select and NOW don't get them.

★

Why start at the top?

Starting on Plus gives you the most value to show and keeps room to come down later if price is a worry. We never start on Select.

PLUS PRO MOVE

A locked phone is an opportunity.

A locked phone is still tied to the old carrier, so it can't move over yet. Don't drop the customer to BYOD. Turn it into a new phone purchase and keep them on Plus.

Intake · Device Row
1

What "locked" means

The phone is still tied to their old carrier, so they can't bring it to us right now. (BYOD means "bring your own device" — keep your current phone.)

2

The move

Instead of BYOD, put them on a new financed phone. They stay on Plus and may qualify for a trade-in or a device promotion.

3

Why it's smart

Going from a locked phone to a new phone keeps the same Plus price. It does not raise the plan. (Moving a customer up from Select to Plus DOES raise the price — this does not.) So they keep every Plus benefit with no price jump.

Locked phone → new phone: same Plus priceSelect → Plus: price goes up
5 Add Devices — Financing & Trade-Ins

Phones, watches, and tablets — priced live.

Intake · Device Financing
1

Tap "Device Financing"

This opens a row for each line. Search the phone and pick the storage. The cost splits over 36 months for you. BYOD is $0 if they keep their phone.

2

TIPS Trade-In

Pick the phone they're trading. The promo applies as bill credits — you can watch a discount like $600 land. The trade is based on the NEW phone they're getting.

3

Watches +$10, Tablets +$20

Add connected devices with one tap. Remember: device deals and trade-ins are Plus only. Select and NOW pay full price — one more reason we start on Plus.

Plus unlocks the deals
6 Build the Comparison & Recap

Let the T-chart do the talking.

Comparison · The T-Chart
"So today you have 4 lines with Verizon at $220 a month. With Xfinity Mobile Plus, those same 4 lines are $196.67. That saves you $68 a month and $820 your first year."
★

Recap their world, then show yours

The left side is their bill in red. The right side is Xfinity. The green cards stack the monthly and yearly savings so the win is clear.

!

Then stop. Ask.

Say "What do you think?" and go quiet. Let them react. Their answer tells you if you are closing or overcoming.

Transparency · The Receipts

Nothing hidden. Show the math.

When a customer wants the details, the line-by-line breakdown proves every dollar. Being open builds trust and closes deals.

Comparison · Line-by-Line & Device Financing
It lays out the price per line, the plan total, the device fees, and every trade-in credit (like −$600) — so the customer can check it themselves.
Total Transparency

Show them both years. No surprises.

Comparison · Year 2 Pricing
1

One tap flips the view

The Year 1 / Year 2 buttons re-price the whole chart. Year 1 shows the promo. Year 2 shows the standard price.

2

Why we show it

If a customer asks what happens after the promo, we show them gladly. Being the honest rep is how you earn the referral.

3

Still a win in Year 2

Even at standard pricing, the comparison usually still wins. Use it to lock in their confidence.

The Offer Ladder · Keep Your Room

Three offers, in order.

Top-down selling means you always have a next move. Work the ladder from the top. Never jump straight to the bottom.

OFFER 1

Plus, every time

Lead with the Plus plan and its benefits and promotions. Most deals close right here.

OFFER 2

Listen & overcome

If they hesitate, hear the real reason for the "no," answer it with the right benefit, and ask again.

OFFER 3

Select one-year offer

As a last move, offer a special one-year introductory rate on Select lines. The price is lower, and it's still a real win.

Because we started on Plus, we still have room at Offer 3. That is how top-down selling helps you close more deals and keep more customers.
Offer 2 · Listen and Overcome

"No" is just a question you haven't answered yet.

Use active listening. Hear the real reason, match it to one benefit, and ask for the sale again.

STEP 1

Hear the real why

Don't argue with the first thing they say. Ask "What's holding you back?" and really listen. The true reason is usually underneath.

STEP 2

Match one benefit

Pick the single feature or promotion that answers their worry. Show it to them on the sheet.

STEP 3

Ask again

Then ask for the sale. Most deals are won on the second ask, not the first.

There are many ways to overcome an objection. The one rule: you must actually try.
Objection Drills · Listen → Answer → Ask Again

Common "no"s and the move that answers them.

They say"I'd have to pay off my phones first."
→
You answer withThe TIPS trade-in. Trade those phones toward a better device for less. The promo helps cover the payoff.
They say"My phone is locked to my carrier."
→
You answer withSell a new phone on Plus. A new phone keeps the same Plus price — no jump — and can add a trade-in or promo.
They say"Switching feels like a hassle or a cost."
→
You answer withCore service savings. Use the bundle savings to cover the switch. Show the combined number at the top.
They say"It still feels like too much."
→
You answer withClimb to Offer 3. Offer the Select one-year member rate — a lower price for their first year.
The sheet is your toolbox. Pick the move that fits the objection in front of you.
Offer 3 · The Member Offer

Our last move: the Select one-year offer.

If Offers 1 and 2 don't land, we lower the price with a special first-year rate on Select lines. We can do this because they are a valued Xfinity member.

"Because you're a valued Xfinity member, I can put your lines on a special one-year introductory rate on our Select plan. It saves you money this first year for signing up, and I'd love to get you set up today."

What it is

A special, lower price on Select lines for their first year.

Why we can offer it

It's a member benefit. Say that plainly and honestly.

Why it's the LAST move

Because we started on Plus, we kept the room to come down to this. That's top-down selling.

Always be transparent. We never hide the terms. Honesty on the last offer is what makes a customer say yes — and come back.
Why Top-Down Wins

Start high so you keep room to close.

Live Savings Tally

When you start on Plus, you always have somewhere to go if the customer hesitates: overcome the objection, then offer the Select one-year rate. If you start low, you have nothing left to give.

Big savings number → room to add a device and still winThin savings number → lean on trade-ins and core savings

Glance at the savings total before every offer. It keeps you from promising too much — and from leaving money on the table.

They said yes

Yes? Write it up.

Comparison · Action Bar
✓

Submit

This logs the quote and the sale. It checks your name and store first, then sends it through, with a retry built in so nothing gets lost.

📧

Email PDF

Sends the customer a clean copy of their comparison to take home. Great for the "let me think about it" follow-up.

🖨

Print

Makes a black-and-white PDF you can hand them on the spot.

THE FALLBACK · SAVE THEM ANYWAY

No mobile? Still save them money.

A customer can say no to moving their phones and still walk out saving every month. You just shift the sheet into Devices-Only Mode and keep selling the core savings.

Devices-Only Mode
1

Tap "Customer Declines Mobile Offer"

This drops the sheet into Devices-Only Mode. The carrier and bill fields become optional, so you keep selling savings.

2

Add one connected device

Add a watch, a tablet, or a home-phone line, and the core bundle savings still show. They walk out saving money even without moving their phones.

3

The rule

Every rebundle needs a mobile product. A watch or tablet counts, and the sheet shows the customer why the bundle makes sense.

THE ONE-SCREEN CHEAT SHEET

Run the whole play from memory.

Here is the full Plus-first play on one screen. Learn these steps cold, then run them in order on every customer.

1
Find core savings

Current bill to bundle bill. Bank the upgrade.

2
Pivot & ask the four

Who / how many lines / what's the bill / why so much.

3
Mirror their account

Carrier, lines, bill, watches, tablets, tier — exactly.

4
Start on Plus

Best plan, most benefits, most promotions.

5
Add devices

Financing, trade-ins, watches, tablets. (Plus unlocks the deals.)

6
Build, recap, stop, ask

Show the T-chart, say the savings, then go quiet.

▲
Work the ladder

Offer Plus, then overcome the real objection, then the Select one-year member offer.

★
Declined? Save them anyway

Add a watch, tablet, or home-phone to keep the core savings.

Start high. Keep your room. Quote from the live sheet, never from memory.
🔥
That's the play

Now Go Cook.

Start on Plus. Show the savings. Listen, answer, and ask again. Keep your room by selling from the top down. The sheet brings the proof — you bring the belief.

Built for the team. By the team. Let's eat. 💰
